contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 120
- Description: AUM: refactor to read Balance Asset section (header-driven, formula-matched)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/306
  
  Details:
  Operator caught the root structural mistake: calculateAUM() was summing every row of each AGL Balance sheet across cols A-B-C — that's the Equity (stakeholder claims) section, not the Asset (what-the-ledger-holds) section. Same per-AGL total when books reconcile but semantically wrong, and structurally risky for double-counting if any stakeholder claim mirrors items also tracked on the Main Ledger off-chain balance.
  
  Three PRs:
  
  1. tokenomics#306 (squash 2a08067, deploy @13)
     - New getManagedLedgerAssetsFromAssetSection() reader with header-driven column detection — AGLs use two different templates (AGL4: Asset at cols A-B; everyone else: Asset at cols E-F). Reader scans row 5 for an 'Asset'/'Asset Name' cell, uses that column + next column as (asset, amount), reads data from row 6+. Each asset converted to USD via Currencies!A → Currencies!B (price in USD per unit).
     - New computeAumBreakdown() returning structured payload mirroring computeTreasuryBreakdown shape
     - PropertiesService cache + doGet?type=aum_breakdown endpoint (with &refresh=1 escape hatch)
     - aum_debug probe endpoint kept post-fix for future template-drift investigation (no new exposure since sheets are already linkable from /treasury and /aum click-throughs)
     - calculateAUM() refactored to derive scalar from computeAumBreakdown in one pass + cache as side effect
     - Live verification: AUM = ,868.36 (off_chain ,442.61 + USDT /bin/zsh + managed ,425.75). Down from old Equity-sum ,251.72 and treasury-cache ,082.13 — both inflated. Per-asset detail clean (AGL15 ,294 USD, AGL8 ,409 cacao+USD+BRL, etc).
  
  2. truesight_me_beta#138 (squash 8fd6dae, prod cherry-pick 6991e05)
     - /aum page switched from treasury-cache to GAS aum_breakdown endpoint
     - Two sections: Main Ledger (off-chain + USDT, with subtotal) and Managed ledgers (per-AGL Asset section with per-asset detail: native qty × Currencies rate = USD)
     - Headline now matches landing-page AUM scalar exactly (will catch up on next updateTotalDAOAssetOnWix cron)
  
  3. agentic_ai_context#175 (squash f03abbb)
     - MANAGED_LEDGER_EXPLORER_PATTERN.md gets the missing Balance template column map: both layouts (canonical Equity A-C / Asset E-F vs older AGL4 Asset A-B / Resource Location D-F), header row 5 / data row 6 convention, and a pointer to which downstream consumer reads which section.
  
  Data hygiene flag for Gary (not a code bug): AGL7's Equity records 'TrueSight DAO: $242.86 USD' but the Asset section is empty (#N/A). DAO injected capital but no asset has been recorded against it yet. Surfaces an accounting gap.
  
  CNAME-safe prod promotion: cherry-picked 8fd6dae onto truesight_me_prod main, rebased through a stats-refresh race, verified CNAME 'truesight.me' intact post-push. NOT 'gh repo sync --force'.
  
  Diagnostic probe path: discovered the column-layout drift by deploying a aum_debug endpoint that dumps the first 20x6 cells of any AGL's Balance sheet. Probed all 13 active managed ledgers — confirmed AGL4 is the lone outlier on the older template, and that the rest use Layout A. Header-driven detection in the reader handles both without per-AGL config.
  
  PRs:
  - TrueSightDAO/tokenomics#306 (squash 2a08067; GAS deploy @13)
  - TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta#138 (squash 8fd6dae; prod cherry-pick 6991e05)
  - TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context#175 (squash f03abbb)
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 200.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
--------
